zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John le Despenser於1235年出生在英國武斯特斯爾德福德時，他的父親Sir Geoffrey le Despenser51歲，母親Emma d'Harcourt30歲。他於大約1260年與Anne de Lou在英國武斯特斯爾德福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1274年在英國維爾爾斯瓦洛雷費爾德享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John le Despenser于1235年出生在英国武斯特斯爾德福德时，他的父亲Sir Geoffrey le Despenser51岁，母亲Emma d'Harcourt30岁。他于大约1260年与Anne de Lou在英国武斯特斯爾德福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1274年在英国維爾爾斯瓦洛雷費爾德享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John le Despenser was born in 1235, in Defford, Worcestershire, England, his father, Sir Geoffrey le Despenser, was 51 and his mother, Emma d'Harcourt, was 30. He married Anne de Lou about 1260, in Defford, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1274, in Swallowfield, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John le Despenser est né en 1235, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Geoffrey le Despenser, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Emma d'Harcourt, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Anne de Lou environ 1260, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1274, à Swallowfield, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John le Despenser 1235 in Defford, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Geoffrey le Despenser 51 und seine Mutter Emma d'Harcourt 30. Er hat um 1260 in Defford, Worcestershire, England Anne de Lou geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1274 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Swallowfield, Wiltshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Despenser è nato il nel 1235 nel Defford, Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir Geoffrey le Despenser, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Emma d'Harcourt, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Anne de Lou circa 1260, nel Defford, Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1274, nel Swallowfield, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John le Despenserが1235年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードで生まれた時，父Sir Geoffrey le Despenserは51歳，母Emma d'Harcourtは30歳であった。約1260年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードでAnne de Louと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1274年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアスワロウフィールドで39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John le Despenser(이)가 1235년 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey le Despenser(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma d'Harcourt(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1260년 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 Anne de Lou(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1274년 영국 윌츠히르 스월로필드에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John le Despenser nasceu em 1235, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Geoffrey le Despenser, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Emma d'Harcourt, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne de Lou aproximadamente 1260, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1274, em Swallowfield, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 39 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John le Despenser родился в 1235 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Geoffrey le Despenser было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Emma d'Harcourt, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne de Lou около 1260 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1274 г. в местности сваллоуфиелд, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 39 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John le Despenser nació en 1235, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Geoffrey le Despenser, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Emma d'Harcourt, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Anne de Lou aproximadamente 1260, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1274, en Swallowfield, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años.
